heading: Recovery and disposition authority
label: 20147 Recovery and disposition authority
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)20147.42 U.S.C. 2459l, as added , , .Pub. L. 85–568, title III, § 317Pub. L. 109–155, title VII, § 705119 Stat. 2936Dec. 30, 2005
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)20147.42 U.S.C. 2459l, as added , , .Pub. L. 85–568, title III, § 317Pub. L. 109–155, title VII, § 705119 Stat. 2936Dec. 30, 2005

children.label: A
children.text: is intended to transport one or more persons;

children.label: B
children.text: is designed to operate in outer space; and

children.label: ii
children.text: owned by an Administration contractor or cooperating party and operated as part of an Administration mission or a joint mission with the Administration.

children.heading: .—Crewmember
children.label: 2 .—Crewmember
children.text: The term “crewmember” means an astronaut or other person assigned to an Administration human space flight vehicle.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (2) and (3), when there is an accident or mishap resulting in the death of a crewmember of an Administration human space flight vehicle, the Administrator may take control over the remains of the crewmember and order autopsies and other scientific or medical tests.

children.heading: .—Treatment
children.label: 2 .—Treatment
children.text: Each crewmember shall provide the Administrator with the crewmember’s preferences regarding the treatment accorded to the crewmember’s remains and the Administrator shall, to the extent possible, respect those stated preferences.

children.heading: .—Construction
children.label: 3 .—Construction
children.text: This section shall not be construed to permit the Administrator to interfere with any Federal investigation of a mishap or accident.
